summary: Integration tests are structurally sound and cover the required scenarios, but two critical cross-file consistency gaps (missing KeepServing/ErrorCheck addons in both test fixtures) mean the tests exercise a different addon chain than production. Four major issues further reduce reliability: the TLS-restart test verifies only TCP connectivity, the HTTP proxy test is network-dependent and always skips offline, a hardcoded port risks port conflicts, and the CA cert fallback path silently fails in fresh environments.

criticalIssues.issue: _start_proxy fixture loads only 6 addons (Core, Proxyserver, NextLayer, TlsConfig, addon, extra_addons), missing KeepServing and ErrorCheck that production _start_mitmproxy loads (src/main.py:365-367: Core, Proxyserver, NextLayer, TlsConfig, KeepServing, ErrorCheck, addon, FlowCleanup).
criticalIssues.impact: Integration tests exercise a different addon chain than production. KeepServing prevents premature proxy exit — without it, the proxy may shut down mid-test unexpectedly. ErrorCheck reports startup errors — without it, misconfigured proxy starts silently. Tests cannot validate the actual 8-addon production setup.
criticalIssues.recommendation: Add KeepServing and ErrorCheck imports to _start_proxy and insert them in the addons list after TlsConfig, matching the exact production order: Core(), Proxyserver(), NextLayer(), TlsConfig(), KeepServing(), ErrorCheck(), addon, *extra_addons.

criticalIssues.issue: TestProxyStartsAndInjects.run_proxy loads Core, Proxyserver, NextLayer, TlsConfig, GeoFixAddon — also missing KeepServing and ErrorCheck versus production addon chain in src/main.py:365-367.
criticalIssues.impact: Same as above: this test (updated from DumpMaster as part of this diff) doesn't replicate the actual production startup, defeating the purpose of the update. The diff's stated goal was to update to minimal Master pattern to exercise production code path.
criticalIssues.recommendation: Add KeepServing and ErrorCheck imports inside run_proxy and include them in master.addons.add call after TlsConfig.

suggestions.suggestion: test_tls_works_after_restart only tests TCP socket connectivity (socket.create_connection), not TLS. The test name, docstring, and tech-spec acceptance criterion all say 'TLS works after restart'. After the second proxy starts, a CONNECT request (as in TestOptimizedProxyHTTPS) would actually verify the TLS/HTTPS pipeline is functional.
suggestions.benefit: Test name matches what is actually verified; satisfies tech-spec AC: 'CA certificate re-generated, re-installed... HTTPS works after restart'.

suggestions.suggestion: test_http_request_proxied depends on external httpbin.org. It always skips in offline CI (confirmed: 1 skipped in test results). A local HTTP server started with http.server.HTTPServer or threading + socketserver would make this test reliable without network access.
suggestions.benefit: The test provides value in all environments, not just when httpbin.org is reachable. Eliminates the only consistently skipped test.

suggestions.suggestion: Hardcoded port 18090 in TestProxyStartsAndInjects.run_proxy. If the port is already in use (parallel test runs, previous test didn't clean up), the test fails with a confusing error. The same file imports socket — _free_port() is already defined in test_integration_proxy.py and should be extracted to a shared location or re-implemented here.
suggestions.benefit: Eliminates flaky port-conflict failures in CI.

suggestions.suggestion: TestCACertificate fallback cert-generation path (lines 135-141) creates a Master with no addons. TlsConfig is what generates the CA cert on startup — without it, the cert will never be created. This path silently fails (bare except) and the test then skips. This was a silent regression introduced when DumpMaster (which loaded TlsConfig as a default addon) was replaced with bare Master.
suggestions.benefit: Ensures CA cert can be generated in fresh environments (e.g., CI first run, new developer machine).

suggestions.suggestion: time.sleep(1.0) in _shutdown_proxy is an arbitrary delay added after loop.stop(). Since _wait_port_free() already polls for port release deterministically (defined at line 40), it could replace the sleep for a tighter shutdown guarantee.
suggestions.benefit: Reduces total test suite time (each test calling _shutdown_proxy waits 1s unnecessarily).

suggestions.suggestion: FakeHeaders/FakeRequest/FakeResponse/FakeFlow are defined inline inside test_js_injected_for_target_domain (lines 177-210), and a similar but different FakeRequest/FakeResponse/FakeFlow triplet is defined in TestFlowCleanup._make_flow (lines 234-248). test_proxy_addon.py already has FakeFlow helpers. Consider extracting shared fakes to a conftest.py or test helper module.
suggestions.benefit: Reduces duplication and inconsistency between fake implementations across test files.

suggestions.suggestion: FakeResponse in TestJSInjection has content (bytes) and text (property) that are not synced — real mitmproxy HTTPResponse keeps them in sync. After addon.response(flow) modifies flow.response.text, flow.response.content still holds the original bytes. After cleanup.response(flow) sets content=b"", text still contains the injected HTML. This doesn't fail the current assertions but makes FakeResponse an inaccurate model of mitmproxy behavior.
suggestions.benefit: More accurate fake object catches potential bugs where GeoFixAddon reads content instead of text.
